task: Classify customer product reviews as positive or negative to help an e-commerce business detect product problems early. Train a binary text classifier and evaluate it.
llm_decisions: Full 7-agent pipeline planned for end-to-end NLP binary classification
Dropped 'overall' column to prevent data leakage; engineered combined_text and helpfulness_ratio
Dataset passed all hard-fail validation checks; warnings for outliers and 8.14:1 class imbalance
Selected 'Positive' as target column and 'classification' as task type
Selected LogisticRegression over RandomForest: ROC-AUC 0.9833 vs 0.9569; class_weight='balanced' for imbalance
Model declared production-ready: accuracy 0.959, ROC-AUC 0.983, minority-class F1 0.82
key_findings: 32,939 Amazon Instant Video reviews; 89.1% positive, 10.9% negative (8.14:1 imbalance)
No missing values after preprocessing; 300 reviewerName nulls filled with 'Unknown'
TF-IDF (1,2)-gram on combined_text is the dominant feature; numeric features add marginal lift
Logistic Regression outperforms RandomForest on all metrics for sparse TF-IDF features
87.2% of negative reviews are correctly flagged; 78.2% precision means ~1 in 5 flags is a false alarm
Model reduces manual review workload by approximately 8-9x compared to reading all reviews
issues: Negative-class precision (0.782) is the weakest metric — 92 false positives among predicted negatives
Class imbalance (~89% positive) drives macro F1 (0.90) below weighted F1 (0.96)
No calibration/threshold tuning applied; decision threshold at 0.5 may not be optimal
Outliers detected in 5 numeric columns (mitigated with RobustScaler)
